English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Whitney Balliett papers
Balliett, Whitney.
6948
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
1941-2006 Correspondence, photographs, printed material Notes, drafts, and writings on jazz criticism and biography. Also, letters to Balliett and photographs. Collection material in English 7178789
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Theo Wolf
Date completed:
May 2011
EAD encoding:
Theo Wolf, May 2011
Date modified:
Theo Wolf, May 2011
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Whitney Balliett was esteemed jazz critic. He graduated from Cornell in 1951, and went on to write for The New Yorker. For nearly 50 years, he contributed to the publication with over 550 articles, and many more unsigned. He is known for his non-technical approach to criticism, using artful adjectives and vivid verbs to frame the music. His writing was more poetry than anything, and reading it, one will find metaphors and synesthetic synopses understandable by even the most uninformed music listener.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Notes, drafts, and writings on jazz criticism and biography. Also, letters to Balliett and photographs. The Whitney Balliett collection houses a variety of papers and correspondences from Balliett's career. There are a number of notebooks containing (nearly illegible) notes of various musician's concerts or recordings. There are also items relating to the New Yorker, including a drawing of Buddy Rich by Balliett that appeared in the magazine. Some notable letters include notes from Alice Toklas, John Updike, and Frank Sinatra. Additionally, there are a number of formerly framed items from the wall of Balliett's office.
Cite As:
Whitney Balliett papers, #6948. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Jazz musicians -- United States -- Biography. Jazz -- History and criticism. Charles, Ray, 1930-2004 Dahl, Roald Gillespie, Dizzy, 1917-1993 Sinatra, Frank, 1915-1998 Toklas, Alice B. Updike, John Newport Jazz Festival

City Voices: Fourteen New York Portraits315Photographs, drawings, postersPhotograph of Duke Ellington in front of the Apollo theater, signed to Balliett with appreciation.Avedon, Richard, 1923-20041963-11-3093 - Scope and Contents
Includes a sheet identifying all people in photograph.Harlem 1958 photograph, also known as A Great Day in Harlem, and a modern recreation photographKane, Art, 1925-04-09-1995-02-031958-200393 - Folder 1
Scope and Contents
The photograph depicts 57 jazz musicians. In the more modern, recreated photograph at the same location, ten of the original musicians are pictured. The photograph was taken sometime before October 2003.Photograph of Ben Webster in silhouetteUndated93 - Folder 1"Red Allen, Pee Wee Russell Quintet - Introduced by Whitney Balliett" performance posterUndated93 - Folder 1Photograph of Balliett with Duke Ellington backstage at the Monterey Jazz Festival.Seligo, Fred, 1935-19691966316
Scope and Contents
Also in the photograph is Nancy and Fred Seligo's date. Fred took the photograph at three a.m.Gelatin silver prints.Photographs316
Scope and Contents
Includes photograph of Balliett at home taken by A. Hall, a New Yorker publication with an interview and photographs titled "Whitney Balliett and All That Jazz", and a photograph of drummer Big Sid Catlett by Skippy Adelman taken circa 1945, printed later.Pencil drawing on paper of Whitney Balliett playing the drums by Paul ResikaJanuary 15, 1991317Watercolor and ink drawing for Atlantic Monthly by Barry Blitt.318
Scope and Contents
The illustration was for a piece Balliett wrote about playing the drums with a New Orleans group on a jazz cruise in November 1994, which no critics were invited on. It was a ten day voyage on the QE2 from New York City to Antigua. ======= Misc.
